title: ADR-022: No spread operators in tight loops on hot-path files
evidence: Grep for spread operators in price-matrix.ts, provider-latency-tracker.ts, runtime-monitor.ts, execution-engine/src/, unified-detector/src/. Spreads found are in getters ({...this.config}, {...this.stats}), constructor initialization, config merging, and cold-path status/metrics methods. Line 313 health-monitoring-manager.ts has push(...trimmedEntries) inside a for-of loop but in a periodic cleanup function (not hot-path). Line 151 circuit-breaker-manager.ts has spread in a for-of loop but getAllStatus() is a status query. No spreads found in hot-path tight loops (price updates, detection, execution).
expected: No spread operators inside for/while/forEach/map/reduce/filter loops on hot-path code
actual: All spread operators on hot-path files are in cold-path or one-shot contexts — compliant with ADR-022

title: ADR-022: RingBuffer uses Float64Array with O(1) record()
evidence: Line 71: buffer field is Float64Array (line 80: this.buffer = new Float64Array(capacity)). record() method (line 83-94) uses direct index assignment (this.buffer[this.index] = value) and modular increment ((this.index + 1) % this.capacity) — all O(1) operations with no allocation.
expected: Float64Array for ring buffer storage, O(1) record method
actual: Confirmed: Float64Array at line 71/80, O(1) record at lines 83-94

title: ADR-033: Stale price window correctly configured per layer
evidence: Cross-chain detector (detector.ts:1486): maxPriceAgeMs defaults to 30000ms via `this.config.maxPriceAgeMs ?? 30000`. PriceMatrix.getPriceWithFreshnessCheck (price-matrix.ts:717): defaults to 5000ms. ADR-033 explicitly documents the 5000ms L1 cache default as intentional (different layer from the 30s cross-chain detection threshold), with per-chain thresholds configurable via caller argument.
expected: Cross-chain detection: 30000ms. L1 cache: 5000ms (intentionally different per ADR-033 table).
actual: Both values match ADR-033 specification exactly

title: ADR-002: No direct HTTP inter-service calls found
evidence: Grep for http://localhost:300[0-6] in services/**/*.ts found only: (1) CORS origin configuration in coordinator middleware (line 86: allowedOrigins default includes localhost:3000/3001) — this is browser CORS, not inter-service communication. (2) Test files (middleware.test.ts). All inter-service communication uses Redis Streams per ADR-002.
expected: No direct HTTP calls between services (Redis Streams only per ADR-002)
actual: No inter-service HTTP calls found; only CORS browser origin config and test files

title: ADR-018: Circuit breaker failureThreshold defaults to 5
evidence: Default values confirmed at: shared/core/src/resilience/circuit-breaker.ts:408 (failureThreshold: 5), shared/core/src/resilience/self-healing-manager.ts:36 (SYSTEM_CONSTANTS?.circuitBreaker?.defaultFailureThreshold ?? 5), services/execution-engine/src/types.ts:683 (failureThreshold: 5), services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:99 (safeParseInt(env, 5)), services/coordinator/src/alerts/notifier.ts:47 (failureThreshold: 5), services/execution-engine/src/strategies/uniswapx-filler.strategy.ts:188 (failureThreshold: 5), shared/core/src/mev-protection/backrun-bundle-builder.ts:183 (failureThreshold: 5). Only exception: mev-share-event-listener.ts:233 uses maxReconnectAttempts as threshold (by design — CB opens when reconnects exhausted).
actual: All defaults confirmed at 5. MEV listener uses dynamic threshold (intentional).

title: ADR-005: SharedArrayBuffer used for primary price storage
evidence: Line 224: private sharedBuffer: SharedArrayBuffer | null. Line 438-439: if typeof SharedArrayBuffer !== 'undefined', this.sharedBuffer = new SharedArrayBuffer(totalBytes). Line 898: 'Optimized SharedArrayBuffer path with Fix #7 sequence counter protocol'. Thread-safe read/write with seqlock pattern documented in file header (lines 15-16). Fallback to ArrayBuffer only when SharedArrayBuffer unavailable.
expected: SharedArrayBuffer used for L1 price storage per ADR-005
actual: Confirmed: SharedArrayBuffer is the primary storage mechanism with seqlock thread safety

title: Fly.io execution-engine health check port 8080 does not match internal_port 3005
evidence: Lines 42: internal_port = 3005. Line 37: HEALTH_CHECK_PORT = '3005'. Lines 70,79: checks use port = 8080. The SA-1Q-001 fix comment (line 35-36) states HEALTH_CHECK_PORT was changed from 8080 to 3005, but the [checks] section was not updated. The app will listen on 3005 but Fly.io health checks will probe port 8080, which has no listener — causing perpetual health check failure in production.
expected: Health check port should match internal_port (3005)
recommendation: Update infrastructure/fly/execution-engine.toml checks to use port = 3005 instead of port = 8080

title: All Dockerfiles use node:22-alpine — consistent
evidence: All 8 service Dockerfiles (coordinator, execution-engine, cross-chain-detector, unified-detector, partition-asia-fast, partition-l2-turbo, partition-high-value, partition-solana) use FROM node:22-alpine. Matches engines >=22.0.0 in package.json.

title: Fly.io port and health check paths align with service-ports.json
evidence: Fly.io internal_port values: coordinator=3000 (/api/health), coordinator-standby=3000 (/api/health), partition-asia-fast=3001 (/health, /ready), partition-l2-turbo=3002 (/health, /ready), partition-high-value=3003 (/health, /ready), partition-solana=3004 (/health, /ready), cross-chain-detector=3006 (/health), execution-engine=3005 (/health, /ready). All match service-ports.json. Coordinator uses /api/health (Express prefix); others use /health or /ready.
actual: All aligned (except execution-engine check port — see SA-4Q1)

title: Docker Compose port mappings and health checks consistent
evidence: docker-compose.yml: coordinator=3000 (/api/health), unified-detector=3001 (/health), cross-chain=3006 (/health), execution-engine=3005 (/health). docker-compose.partition.yml: All partitions use internal port 3001 (containerized, host-mapped 3011-3016), /health checks correct. docker-compose.testnet.yml: l2-turbo=3002, high-value=3003, cross-chain=3006, execution-engine=3005, coordinator=3000 — all match. All health check intervals standardized to 15s per ADR-007.
expected: Ports and health paths consistent across compose files

title: Coordinator shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: shutdownTimeoutMs=15000 (index.ts:194). Redis connectTimeout=3000ms (shared/core/src/redis/client.ts:240). closeServerWithTimeout default=5000ms. 15000 > 5000 (server close) and 15000 > 3000 (Redis connect). Hierarchy: shutdown(15s) > server-close(5s) > redis-connect(3s).

title: Execution engine shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: Total shutdownTimeoutMs = drainTimeoutMs(30000) + POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER_MS(15000) = 45000ms (index.ts:446). SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 (engine.ts:805). Per-disconnect SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=8000 (types.ts:868). Redis connectTimeout=3000ms. Hierarchy: total-shutdown(45s) > drain(30s) > per-disconnect(8s) > redis-connect(3s). SA-1R-005 validates hierarchy at startup.

title: Cross-chain detector shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: Service-level shutdownTimeoutMs=10000 (default from setupServiceShutdown). Per-disconnect timeout=4000ms (detector.ts:515). Two sequential disconnects: 2*4000=8000ms < 10000ms. Redis connectTimeout=3000ms. 10000 > 8000 (total internal) > 4000 (per-disconnect) > 3000 (redis-connect).

title: Partition services shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=8000 (line 501). Used for both closeServerWithTimeout (line 585) and detector.stop() timeout (line 598). Sequential: 8000+8000=16000ms max. Redis connectTimeout=3000ms. 8000 > 3000 (shutdown step > redis-connect). No outer force-exit timer — each step calls process.exit() directly on timeout.
actual: 8000 > 3000 — consistent. No outer force-exit timer (exits directly per-step).

title: Monolith service shutdown timeout is hardcoded fallback
evidence: monolith/src/index.ts:66: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS = safeParseInt(process.env.MONOLITH_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS, 30000). monolith/src/worker-manager.ts:113: this.shutdownTimeoutMs = config.shutdownTimeoutMs ?? 30_000. Both default to 30000ms — configurable via env. The worker-manager per-worker timeout (line 359) is the same as the overall timeout, meaning if one worker hangs, the others may not get shutdown time.
actual: Per-worker timeout (30s) equals overall shutdown timeout (30s) — a slow worker could consume the entire budget
recommendation: Consider making the per-worker shutdown timeout a fraction of the overall timeout (e.g., shutdownTimeoutMs / maxWorkers) to ensure all workers get a chance to shut down
